Name: Robin
Biography: Damian Wayne, the son of Bruce Wayne and Talia al Ghul, was raised by the League of Assassins before being brought to Gotham City to live with his father. As Robin, he served as Batman's partner and has developed into a skilled detective and martial artist. Since the war ended, he returned to lead the League of Assassins.

Name: Nightwing
Biography: Dick Grayson was the first Robin and later became Nightwing, protector of Blüdhaven and a founding member of the Titans. Prior to the invasion he was considered one of the world's greatest heroes and leaders. When Earth fell, Dick helped organize resistance efforts across multiple cities. Over two decades of occupation he became one of the most respected leaders of the underground movement, coordinating operations against Apokoliptian forces throughout North America.

Name: Red Hood
Biography: Jason Todd was Batman's second Robin before his death at the hands of the Joker. Resurrected by the Lazarus Pit, he returned as the Red Hood and frequently clashed with Gotham's heroes over his lethal methods. During the invasion he proved unexpectedly invaluable, using his willingness to cross moral lines to keep resistance networks alive. Today he leads high-risk raids against prisons, laboratories, and collaborators across occupied territories.

Name: Red Robin
Biography: Tim Drake deduced Batman's identity as a child and eventually became the third Robin. Widely considered one of the greatest detectives of his generation, Tim later adopted the identity of Red Robin. Following the invasion, he became a master intelligence coordinator, building hidden communication networks and resistance cells. His current location remains one of the Resistance's most closely guarded secrets.

Name: Oracle
Biography: Barbara Gordon began her career as Batgirl before transitioning into the role of Oracle after suffering a life-changing injury. Prior to the invasion she served as the information hub for countless heroes and teams. After Earth fell, Oracle became one of the most important figures in the Resistance, maintaining hidden communications networks and coordinating operations across the globe while remaining hidden from Apokoliptian surveillance.

Name: Batman
Biography: Bruce Wayne dedicated his life to protecting Gotham City after witnessing the murder of his parents. As Batman he became one of the founding members of the Justice League and one of Earth's greatest heroes. During Darkseid's conquest he fought until the very end. His body was taken by Apokoliptian forces.

Name: Arsenal
Biography: Roy Harper was Green Arrow's longtime partner and one of the Titans' most accomplished members. Despite struggles with addiction and personal loss, Roy repeatedly rebuilt his life and continued fighting for others. Following the invasion he went into hiding with his family. His whereabouts are unknown after his capture years ago.

Name: Green Arrow
Biography: Oliver Queen was once a billionaire playboy who reinvented himself as Green Arrow after surviving on a remote island. Known for his sharp political views and unwavering dedication to ordinary people, Oliver became one of the loudest voices opposing injustice. When Earth fell, he transformed Star City into a center of resistance activity and has spent twenty years leading one of the largest anti-Apokolips movements on the planet.

Name: The Flash
Biography: Wally West began as Kid Flash before eventually succeeding Barry Allen as the Flash. Prior to the invasion he was regarded as one of the fastest beings in existence and a cornerstone of both the Titans and Justice League. During the war he died in a battle with Apokoliptian forces.

Name: Raven
Biography: Born as the daughter of the demon Trigon, Raven spent much of her life struggling to control the darkness within her. She became a founding Titan and repeatedly helped save the world from supernatural threats. After the invasion she disappeared from public view, leading to countless rumors regarding her fate. Resistance legends claim she secretly protects hidden settlements from both demonic and Apokoliptian forces.

Name: Beast Boy
Biography: Garfield Logan gained the ability to transform into animals after surviving a rare illness as a child. As Beast Boy he became a beloved member of the Titans, known for his humor and compassion. He fought for the Resistance until his capture by Apokoliptian forces.

Name: Granny Goodness
Biography: Granny Goodness is one of Darkseid's oldest and most loyal servants. For ages she has overseen the brutal orphanages of Apokolips, producing elite warriors and loyal followers. Following Earth's conquest, she established moren of these orphanages on Earth. Many Sentinels and Proto-Furies were trained by her and her daughters.

Name: Kalibak
Biography: Kalibak is the brutal son of Darkseid and one of Apokolips' most feared generals. Long before Earth's conquest he led campaigns against countless worlds.

Name: The Riddler
Biography: Edward Nygma built his criminal career around proving his intellectual superiority through elaborate puzzles and schemes. One of Batman's oldest adversaries, he spent years testing Gotham's heroes. During the occupation he reinvented himself as a broker of information, selling secrets to resistance fighters, criminals, and Apokoliptian officials alike.

Name: Penguin
Biography: Oswald Cobblepot rose from Gotham's criminal underworld to become one of its most influential crime bosses. Prior to the invasion he maintained a careful balance between legitimate business and organized crime. When Earth fell, Penguin adapted quickly, building profitable relationships with collaborators and black-market networks while preserving his own power.

Name: Two-Face
Biography: Harvey Dent's fractured psyche became even more unstable under occupation. His decisions are still determined by the flip of a coin.

Name: Scarecrow
Biography: Crane willingly cooperated with Apokoliptian researchers, helping develop methods of suppressing resistance through terror.

Name: Mr. Freeze
Biography: Victor Fries remains focused on preserving Nora. He rarely involves himself in larger conflicts unless her safety is threatened.

Name: Poison Ivy
Biography: As Earth's ecosystems suffered under Apokoliptian rule, Ivy became increasingly militant. Entire forests now answer to her will.

Name: Bane
Biography: Bane survived the invasion by consolidating power in isolated regions. Few challenge his authority directly.

Name: Talon
Biography: Commander in the Sentinels of Unity. His sector of control includes the Western United States and Canada.
